title: Telis laciniata
taxonomy: Telis laciniata is classified as a species of moth in the family Crambidae. It belongs to the order Lepidoptera, which comprises butterflies and moths. Within the family Crambidae, Telis laciniata is placed in the subfamily Crambinae. This classification is based on the characteristics of the moth's physical features and its behavior. Further classification details may be available in specific taxonomic resources or databases.
properties: Telis laciniata is reportedly used in traditional medicine, and it is said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. It is believed to help alleviate symptoms of various health issues, including fever and digestive problems. The plant is also thought to have antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of infections. Additionally, Telis laciniata is said to have immunomodulatory properties, which could help regulate the immune system. However, further research is needed to confirm the therapeutic properties of Telis laciniata.
